key_insight: The paper proposes a reduced-integration virtual element formulation with scaled boundary parametrization that uses analytical section integration and only one integration point per section to stabilize finite-strain problems.
engineering_relevance: This can make virtual element methods more efficient and robust for large-deformation solid mechanics problems, including hyperelastic and elasto-plastic response in complex geometries.
method: Expand constitutive quantities in a Taylor series about the sectional center.
Use scaled boundary parametrization to enable analytical integration of the weak form.
Reduce quadrature to a single integration point per section while retaining stabilization.
evidence_signal: The formulation was evaluated on several numerical examples, including a nonlinear patch test, large-deformation compression, hyperelastic anisotropy, and elasto-plasticity, with generally good agreement in capturing structural response.
watch_item: The patch test was not satisfied for higher-order shape functions, and performance depended on how closely the physical element matched the assumed parent element geometry.
paper_title: Reduced integration with scaled boundary parametrization for virtual elements at finite strains
